commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `scopeEnvelope` session metadata, exposes it through `sessions.patch`, preserves it across session-backed runs, and passes it to `before_prompt_build` hooks with docs and tests.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +236, Docs +2, Generated 0, Other +8. Total +378 across 26 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. Source inspection does show the current branch conflicts with current main and applies the central changes to paths that no longer own the live runner/protocol behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should decide the new plugin API/session-state contract before repair; if sponsored, the branch needs a focused rebase onto current runner and protocol paths.

target.title: feat: pass scopeEnvelope to before_prompt_build hooks
target.body:
## Summary
- add a typed `PromptBuildScopeEnvelope` session metadata object
- persist/patch `scopeEnvelope` through session state and gateway session patch APIs
- pass `scopeEnvelope` into `before_prompt_build` so prompt-build hooks can receive structured workspace/task scope without parsing prompt text

## Validation
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `before_prompt_build` hooks now receive session `scopeEnvelope` metadata across session-backed paths, including isolated cron embedded-agent runs, while docs/changelog/plugin SDK API metadata stay in sync.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node v22.22.0 and pnpm 10.29.3, repaired PR branch based on current upstream main. Secrets, user identifiers, private paths, and deployment-specific names omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Installed dependencies with `pnpm install`.
  2. Ran focused agent runner, gateway session patch, auto-reply, cron session-key, and cron owner-auth tests.
  3. Ran core test type checks and plugin SDK API checks after regenerating the SDK API baseline.
  4. Built the repaired branch.
  5. Started the built gateway locally with `node dist/index.js gateway --port <redacted-test-port> --allow-unconfigured` and queried `/health` with `curl`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.owner-auth.test.ts
Test Files  6 passed
Tests  290 passed

$ pnpm check:test-types
completed successfully

$ pnpm check:changed
completed successfully

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

$ pnpm build
completed successfully
```

```text
$ node dist/index.js gateway --port <redacted-test-port> --allow-unconfigured
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:<redacted-test-port>/health
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

- Observed result after fix: The repaired branch forwards the cron session entry into embedded-agent runs, keeps `scopeEnvelope` in the plugin/session surface, passes the focused regression coverage and SDK API check, and starts a built gateway that reports live health.
- What was not tested: Live third-party plugin hooks in a production channel were not exercised; validation was focused in-repo runtime coverage, full changed-file gate, and built-gateway health smoke.
target.author: nikolafc
